title: Mitigate cold starts on scale-up
description: Pre-warm replicas and keep weights resident so a newly scaled instance does not add model-load latency to live requests.
body: Use when a serving deployment scales out or restarts. Do not mark a replica ready before its model is loaded and a warm-up inference has run — the first real requests then pay seconds of load and lazy-init latency. Actions: load weights and run a synthetic warm-up at startup, gate readiness on warm-up completion, and keep a warm headroom of replicas for bursts. Validation signals include the first post-scale request meeting the latency SLO and a readiness probe tied to warm-up. Risk boundary: cold-start latency on every scale event silently violates the SLO during exactly the traffic spikes that triggered scaling; pre-warm before serving.
